description_long: This is a lifelike representation of a Mallard duck with its wings fully extended, as if in mid-flight. The duck features a glossy green head, a yellow bill, and a white ring around its neck, typical of a male Mallard. Its body is covered in brown and gray feathers, with a lighter underbelly and a distinct blue speculum bordered by white on the wings. The legs and webbed feet are orange, and the tail feathers are fanned out, showing a mix of white and gray. The open beak and forward gaze give the impression of vocalization or alertness. The overall form is dynamic, capturing the energy and grace of a duck in motion.
description: A detailed Mallard duck with green head, yellow bill, and outstretched wings, appearing as if in flight. The feathers are brown, gray, and white, with orange webbed feet and a lively, open-beaked expression.
description_view_0: Front view showing the duck's green head, yellow bill, open beak, and fully extended wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the head, open beak, and layered wing feathers.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the fanned tail, back, and symmetrical outstretched wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the head, open beak, and detailed wing and tail feathering.
